facts.fact: The insurer is Pinnacle Investment Life Corp.
The policy number is PIL-VUL-2024-5521.
The policy date is April 5, 2024.
The insured's name is Dr. Amanda Katherine Foster.
Dr. Amanda Katherine Foster's date of birth is September 3, 1975.
Dr. Amanda Katherine Foster's gender is Female.
Dr. Amanda Katherine Foster's age at issue is 48.
Dr. Amanda Katherine Foster's underwriting class is Preferred Plus.
The initial face amount is $1,000,000.
The planned annual premium is $12,000.
The death benefit option selected is Option II (Face + Account Value).
The primary beneficiary is Michael Thomas Foster (Spouse) at 100%.
The secondary beneficiary is Foster Children's Trust at 100%.
Sub-Account is defined as a segregated investment account that invests in shares of a designated mutual fund.
Unit Value is defined as the value of one unit of a sub-account, calculated daily based on fund performance.
Accumulation Units are defined as units held in sub-accounts before any distributions.
Mortality and Expense Risk Charge (M&E) is defined as a daily charge of 0.90% annually to cover insurance risks and administrative costs.
Premium payments (after premium load) are allocated to sub-accounts based on investment instructions.
Available sub-accounts are: Equity Growth Fund, Balanced Allocation Fund, Government Bond Fund, International Equity Fund, Money Market Fund, and Real Estate Securities Fund.
The Money Market Fund has a guaranteed minimum return of 1.5%.
Current allocation is: Equity Growth Fund 40%, Balanced Allocation Fund 30%, Government Bond Fund 20%, Money Market Fund 10%.
The policy owner may change sub-account allocations at any time by written request or through the online portal.
The first 12 allocation changes per year are free; a $25 fee applies thereafter.
The premium load is 5.0% of each premium payment.
The Mortality & Expense Risk Charge is 0.90% per annum of account value, deducted daily.
The administrative charge is $15 per month.
The Cost of Insurance monthly charge is calculated as: (Face Amount at Risk) x (Monthly COI Rate) / 1,000.
Face Amount at Risk is defined as Death Benefit minus Account Value.
Each sub-account has its own expense ratio ranging from 0.45% to 1.25% annually as detailed in the prospectus.
The surrender charge schedule applies as a percentage of premiums paid: Years 1-2: 7%, Years 3-4: 5%, Years 5-6: 3%, Year 7+: 0%.
Under Option I (Level Death Benefit), the death benefit equals the Face Amount.
Under Option II (Variable Death Benefit, currently selected), the death benefit equals the Face Amount plus Account Value.
The policy owner may switch between death benefit options subject to proof of insurability for increases.
Switching death benefit options is subject to minimum face amount requirements.
Switching death benefit options requires a $50 administrative fee.
Under Option II, the account value and death benefit are NOT guaranteed and will fluctuate based on sub-account performance.
Investment risk is borne entirely by the policy owner.
Poor investment performance can cause the policy to lapse if account value becomes insufficient to cover charges.
The Money Market sub-account has a guaranteed minimum rate of 1.5%.
Sub-accounts other than the Money Market have no guaranteed minimum return.
The maximum policy loan is 90% of surrender value.
The policy loan interest rate is 5.5% per annum (fixed).
Loan amounts are moved to the Money Market sub-account.
Policy loans reduce the death benefit dollar-for-dollar.
The minimum partial withdrawal is $1,000.
The maximum partial withdrawal is account value minus $5,000.
The processing fee for a partial withdrawal is $50.
Surrender charges may apply to partial withdrawals.
Partial withdrawals reduce face amount proportionally.
If account value is insufficient to cover monthly deductions for 61 consecutive days, the policy will lapse.
Lapse notice will be sent at least 30 days before lapse.
Reinstatement within 3 years requires evidence of insurability.
Reinstatement requires payment of all back charges plus 6% interest.
Reinstatement requires repayment of outstanding loans.
If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, the insurer pays only premiums paid minus withdrawals.
Death while serving in military conflict in a war zone is excluded if it occurs within 24 months of issue.
Death resulting from operating an aircraft as pilot or crew member is excluded unless declared and approved with extra premium.
This policy is issued in connection with Pinnacle Investment Life Separate Account A, a registered unit investment trust.
The prospectus for the Separate Account and underlying funds must be read carefully before investing.
This is a securities product subject to SEC regulation.
The policy was executed in New York, New York.
The authorized signatory is the President & CEO of Pinnacle Investment Life Corp.
The execution date is April 5, 2024.
Michael Thomas Foster's relationship to the insured is Spouse.
The Equity Growth Fund is one of the available sub-accounts.
The Balanced Allocation Fund is one of the available sub-accounts.
The Government Bond Fund is one of the available sub-accounts.
The International Equity Fund is one of the available sub-accounts.
The Real Estate Securities Fund is one of the available sub-accounts.
The surrender charge for Years 1-2 is 7% of premiums paid.
The surrender charge for Years 3-4 is 5% of premiums paid.
The surrender charge for Years 5-6 is 3% of premiums paid.
There is no surrender charge from Year 7 onwards.
The contestability and suicide period resets upon reinstatement.
The grace period before lapse due to insufficient account value is 61 consecutive days.
Reinstatement must occur within 3 years of lapse.
The back-charge interest rate for reinstatement is 6%.
The war exclusion applies only within 24 months of issue.
The aviation exclusion can be waived if declared and approved with extra premium.
The suicide exclusion also applies within 2 years of reinstatement, not just issue.
